## Image Analysis

This image does **not appear to be an Epic EHR Go-Live tip sheet or screenshot**.

---

### What I See:

This is the **Epic Systems Corporation logo** — the company's brand icon/favicon consisting of:

- An **orange oval/ellipse** shape
- A **white stylized "e" or rocket/bird shape** in the center
- A **white orbital ring** (Saturn-like ring) encircling the oval, suggesting innovation/orbit

---

### Visible Text:
**None** — There is no text in this image.

### Workflow Steps / Buttons / Menu Paths:
**None** — This is purely a logo/brand mark.

Example Domain

Example Domain  This domain is for use in documentation examples without needing permission. Avoid use in operations.

Learn more

Epic Systems — Company Overview & Go-Live Reference
Source: https://www.epic.com/about/

MISSION
Epic develops software to help people get well, stay well, and help future generations be healthier. Their EHR chronicles the story of a patient's healthcare over time.

SCALE & REACH
- Founded 1979, started with 3 half-time employees
- 325+ million patients have active electronic records in Epic
- #1 Overall Software Suite in KLAS for 16 consecutive years
- Invests 35% of operating expenses in R&D
- Employee-owned and developer-led, no acquisitions

HEALTHCARE SETTINGS EPIC SERVES
- Academic medic

Employee-owned and developer-led, no acquisitions

HEALTHCARE SETTINGS EPIC SERVES
- Academic medical centers
- Children's organizations and pediatric hospitals
- Community hospitals
- Dental clinics
- Hospice organizations
- Independent practices
- Mental health facilities
- Rehab centers
- Skilled nursing facilities
- Urgent care and retail clinics
- Specialty and diagnostic labs
- Payers and integrated delivery networks

PATIENT ENGAGEMENT
Epic extends beyond the hospital into patients' homes and mobile devices — enabling remote access and engagement tools (MyChart, patient portals).

COMM

s and mobile devices — enabling remote access and engagement tools (MyChart, patient portals).

COMMUNITY & TRAINING RESOURCES
Epic hosts multiple user group meetings:
- UGM (User Group Meeting)
- XGM, EGM, APGM regional meetings
Training, efficiency resources, and integration technology available via Open@Epic platform.

GO-LIVE RELEVANCE
- Epic is used across all major healthcare settings — know the environment before the floor
- 325M+ patient records means high-volume, high-stakes data integrity
- R&D investment means frequent upgrades — tip sheets and training materials can go stale quickl

ity
- R&D investment means frequent upgrades — tip sheets and training materials can go stale quickly
- MyChart is a key patient-facing module — activation issues are common Go-Live floor questions
- KLAS #1 ranking means high client expectations for uptime and performance

Epic MyChart -- Patient Portal Complete Reference
Source: https://www.epic.com/mychart/

MYCHART OVERVIEW
MyChart is Epic's patient portal enabling healthcare engagement across the full care journey. Patients create a single login through MyChart Central -- one Epic ID works across multiple healthcare organizations.

KEY FEATURES
- Appointment scheduling: online self-scheduling, digital check-in, automatic notifications when earlier slots open
- Health records: real-time viewing of test results, health history, preventive care info, personalized health insights
- Financial tools: online bill p

health history, preventive care info, personalized health insights
- Financial tools: online bill pay, connections to financial assistance programs
- Messaging: secure messaging with care team across multiple channels
- Prescriptions: refill requests, medication lists
- Video visits and telehealth integration

USAGE STATS (Last 12 Months)
- 6.45 billion unique logins
- 195+ million active users
- 140 million appointments scheduled
- 400 million appointments checked in digitally
- Over $16.3 billion collected electronically

SPECIALIZED MYCHART APPS
MyChart Bedside: Hospital inpatients access

lion collected electronically

SPECIALIZED MYCHART APPS
MyChart Bedside: Hospital inpatients access real-time health info, care plans, care team details, and education resources during their stay.
MyChart Care Companion: Home monitoring -- notifications, guidance, integration with home devices to support care plan adherence after discharge.
Hello World: Self-service capabilities including appointment confirmations and bill pay in patients preferred language.

GO-LIVE FLOOR TIPS FOR MYCHART
- Most common Go-Live issue: patients cannot activate MyChart account (activation token problems, email n

t common Go-Live issue: patients cannot activate MyChart account (activation token problems, email not received)
- Proxy access setup for caregivers managing family members
- MyChart Central allows patients to link accounts from multiple Epic organizations
- Digital check-in replaces paper forms -- coach patients to check in via app before arriving
- Push notifications must be enabled on patients phone for best experience
- MyChart Bedside runs on tablets provided at bedside -- charge and clean daily

Epic Interoperability and Data Exchange Reference
Source: https://www.epic.com/interoperability/

CARE EVERYWHERE
Epic's primary patient data exchange platform. Facilitates over 20 million patient records daily. Approximately 50% exchanged with non-Epic EHR systems.

DATA EXCHANGE STATS
- 846 million records exchanged monthly
- 28 million patient documents shared daily (half with non-Epic systems)
- 54.3 billion web service API transactions annually
- 291 billion interface transactions yearly

KEY INTEROPERABILITY TOOLS
Care Everywhere: Pull outside records from other hospitals and health syst

EY INTEROPERABILITY TOOLS
Care Everywhere: Pull outside records from other hospitals and health systems directly into the Epic chart. Providers can view prior notes, labs, imaging from external orgs.
Community Connect: Extends Epic EHR access to independent providers and smaller organizations. Smaller clinics use the hub organizations Epic system.
Community Link: Community users (non-Epic orgs) can access patient charts, place orders and referrals, receive care update notifications -- improves referral continuity.
Patient-Directed Exchange: FHIR APIs let patients send their own records to thir

eferral continuity.
Patient-Directed Exchange: FHIR APIs let patients send their own records to third-party apps they choose.

STANDARDS AND APIS
- 1,000+ publicly available API and interface specs at open.epic.com
- FHIR R4 compliant
- Connections to SSA, DoD, VA for government health data
- Electronic referral system integrations

EPIC NEXUS (TEFCA)
TEFCA-qualified health information network. Connected 625 hospitals since December 2023. Enables secure nationwide record sharing.

GO-LIVE FLOOR TIPS
- Care Everywhere must be enabled per patient -- coaches should know how to pull external recor

TIPS
- Care Everywhere must be enabled per patient -- coaches should know how to pull external records
- Community Connect clients share same environment but may have different workflows
- Referral workflows vary by org -- confirm with build team at Go-Live
- Interoperability issues (missing outside records) are common floor questions -- check Care Everywhere status

Epic AI Features and Tools -- 2025 Reference
Source: https://www.epic.com/ai/

EPIC AI PRODUCT SUITE
Epic has launched AI assistants across three domains:

1. EMMIE (for Patients)
Patient-facing AI assistant. Enhances patient engagement inside MyChart. Answers questions, guides patients through their care journey, helps with navigation.

2. ART (for Clinicians)
Clinical AI documentation tool. Drafts end-of-shift notes, progress notes, and clinical summaries from ambient listening and structured data.
- Nurses write notes 85% faster with Art
- Reduces documentation burden significantly
- Suppor

data.
- Nurses write notes 85% faster with Art
- Reduces documentation burden significantly
- Supports ambient AI charting (listens to patient encounter, auto-generates note)

3. PENNY (for Revenue Cycle and Operations)
AI automation for billing, prior auth, denial management, and operational workflows.
- Fewer claim denials
- Faster prior authorization processing
- Automated coding assistance

AI-ASSISTED DOCUMENTATION (AI CHARTING)
Epic integrates GPT-4 and other large language models in a HIPAA-compliant manner:
- Personalized patient message responses
- Clinical handoff summaries (auto-gen

AA-compliant manner:
- Personalized patient message responses
- Clinical handoff summaries (auto-generated)
- After-visit summaries

PREDICTIVE ANALYTICS -- CURIOSITY
Epic's Curiosity tool uses AI trained on EHR data for predictive medicine:
- Early disease detection
- Preventive care triggers
- Risk stratification

KEY OUTCOMES REPORTED BY EPIC
- Faster documentation
- Earlier diagnoses
- Fewer insurance denials
- Improved patient experiences
- Clinicians spend more time with patients, less on screens

GO-LIVE FLOOR TIPS -- AI FEATURES
- AI Charting (ambient) requires microphone access -- ver

screens

GO-LIVE FLOOR TIPS -- AI FEATURES
- AI Charting (ambient) requires microphone access -- verify device permissions at Go-Live
- Staff may be skeptical of AI notes -- emphasize they must review and sign, AI drafts only
- Emmie in MyChart is patient-facing -- if patients ask who am I chatting with, it is AI
- Penny automates prior auth -- help revenue cycle staff understand new workflow vs old manual process
- These are 2024-2025 rollout features -- not all orgs have them live yet at Go-Live

Epic Open APIs and Developer Platform -- 2025 Reference
Source: https://open.epic.com

OPEN EPIC OVERVIEW
Epic home for data sharing and developer tools. Enables third-party apps and health systems to integrate with Epic.

SCALE
- 8.91 billion patient records exchanged annually
- 623 billion annual interface transactions
- 273 billion web service transactions via public APIs
- 2,751 live applications using Open Epic web services

FHIR APIS
- 750+ no-cost FHIR APIs available
- FHIR R4 standard (working toward USCDI v5)
- May 2025 additions: Observation (DICOM Image Characteristics) for imaging

orking toward USCDI v5)
- May 2025 additions: Observation (DICOM Image Characteristics) for imaging metadata (CT dose, image quality)

REGULATORY APIS (2025)
- 50+ APIs supporting CMS-0057 Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule
- 12 new FHIR APIs for radiation oncology treatment summaries (XRTS standard)

OPERATIONAL APIS
- AGL TelephoneAlert API: Call center agents control phone systems from inside Hyperspace

DEVELOPER RESOURCES
- Step-by-Step Developer Guide
- 40+ Data Sharing Playbooks for specific integration use cases
- Technical specs and API documentation at open.epic.com

aybooks for specific integration use cases
- Technical specs and API documentation at open.epic.com
- Epic Software access for consultants

INDIVIDUAL ACCESS SERVICES (IAS)
Enables secure patient record retrieval. Approximately 800 patient-facing apps live. Patients can connect their health records to apps like Apple Health, Google Health.

GO-LIVE FLOOR TIPS
- Third-party app connections (Apple Health, etc.) use FHIR APIs
- CMS-0057 compliance means prior auth APIs are live or coming soon at most orgs
- Radiation oncology staff may ask about XRTS export for treatment summaries -- it is now su

st orgs
- Radiation oncology staff may ask about XRTS export for treatment summaries -- it is now supported
